text: A photo of a Whirlpool dishwasher model showing the control panel and front face. It is a stainless steel model and features a sleek design.
Another photo of a Whirlpool dishwasher. This model is similar in design to the first image but potentially with slight differences in the control panel layout or other subtle features.

The description that the manual is for 18" & 24" ADA Models Built-In Dishwashers.

The document code, KD-18

Forward: This section introduces the service manual, specifying its purpose and intended audience.  It clarifies that the wiring diagram is for training only and directs users to product-specific documentation for operation and installation details.

Goals and Objectives: This section states the manual's goal of providing information for diagnosing and repairing malfunctions in the specified dishwasher models.  It lists the objectives: understanding safety precautions, troubleshooting and diagnosing malfunctions, performing repairs, and returning the dishwasher to proper operational status.
Disclaimer: Whirlpool Corporation assumes no responsibility for repairs performed by unauthorized personnel.
Copyright Information: Copyright information for Whirlpool Corporation, including the year and location.
Title of the document: "18" & 24" ADA Built-In Dishwashers" and the title of the table of contents
Table of contents, with two columns: one for the section and subsection titles, and one for the page numbers. The table has multiple rows, each representing a different section or subsection.

Table of Contents (continued) for 18" & 24" ADA Built-In Dishwashers, Section 4: Component Access.  Lists procedures for removing various components with corresponding page numbers. Includes a section on Under Tub Components and Notes.

Filtration System and Maintenance section, describes the dishwasher's filtration system, its components (coarse filter, fine filter, lower filter), and how to clean the filters.  It also includes important notes about avoiding damage and proper cleaning techniques.
Diagram showing the three parts of the dishwasher's filter system: coarse filter, fine filter, and lower filter.
Drying - Rinse aid is essential section, explains the importance of using rinse aid for optimal drying performance and avoiding excessive moisture.
Diagram illustrating how to add rinse aid to the dishwasher.
Press START every time you add a dish section, provides instructions on restarting the dishwasher if the door is opened after starting.
Adjust Upper Rack section, details how to adjust the upper rack height.

Dishwasher Care section, explains how to clean the exterior and interior of the dishwasher, including recommendations for detergents and cleaning products, and instructions for reducing the risk of property damage during periods of non-use.
Diagram showing the location of the drain air gap and how to check for blockages.
To Reduce Risk of Property Damage During Vacation or Extended Time Without Use section, provides instructions for protecting the dishwasher from freezing damage during extended periods without use.

Cutout dimensions for the underside of the countertop to the floor.  Dimensions include 24" (61 cm) min width, 24" (61 cm) min depth, and 32 1/2" (82.5 cm) min height.
Note: Route the water supply line and power cable through the channels provided under the dishwasher (circled above).

Underside of countertop to floor: The enclosure must be at least 24" (61 cm) wide, 24" (61 cm) deep, and 32 1/2" (82.5 cm) high. For the front door of the dishwasher to be flush with the leading edge of the counter top, the counter top must be at least 24" (61 cm) deep. This dishwasher is designed to be enclosed on the top and on both sides by a standard residential kitchen cabinet unit. The installation enclosure must be clean and free of any obstructions.
Note: ADA installation, 32 1/2" (82.5 cm) beneath 34" (86.4 cm) high countertops may be accomplished by adjusting the toekick and leveling legs.

Diagnostics & Troubleshooting Safety

Danger sign: Electrical Shock Hazard. Only authorized technicians should perform diagnostic voltage measurements. After performing voltage measurements, disconnect power before servicing. Failure to follow these instructions can result in death or electrical shock.
Warning sign: Electrical Shock Hazard. Disconnect power before servicing. Replace all parts and panels before operating. Failure to do so can result in death or electrical shock.

Voltage Measurement Safety Information. When performing live voltage measurements, you must do the following: Verify the controls are in the off position so that the appliance does not start when energized. Allow enough space to perform the voltage measurements without obstructions. Keep other people a safe distance away from the appliance to prevent potential injury. Always use the proper testing equipment. After voltage measurements, always disconnect power before servicing.

IMPORTANT: Electrostatic Discharge (ESD) Sensitive Electronics. ESD problems are present everywhere. Most people begin to feel an ESD discharge at approximately 3000V. It takes as little as 10V to destroy, damage, or weaken the main control assembly. The new main control assembly may appear to work well after repair is finished, but a malfunction may occur at a later date due to ESD stress.  Instructions on how to avoid ESD damage, using an anti-static wrist strap or touching a green ground connection point repeatedly.

IMPORTANT SAFETY NOTICE ""For Technicians only"". This service data sheet is intended for use by persons having electrical, electronic, and mechanical experience and knowledge at a level generally considered acceptable in the appliance repair trade. Any attempt to repair a major appliance may result in personal injury and property damage. The manufacturer or seller cannot be responsible, nor assume any liability for injury or damage of any kind arising from the use of this data sheet.

A front view of a Whirlpool dishwasher showing the control panel with buttons for various functions such as Cycles, Options, Delay, Start, Cancel, Add Rinse Aid, and Complete.  The display shows "888".

Instructions on how to enter service mode for the dishwasher: turn the dishwasher off, disconnect and reconnect the power, then press and hold both the Cycles and Start/Cancel buttons within 60 seconds

A table detailing the Service Mode Chart.  It lists the component (Water Valve, Wash Pump Heater, High Wash Pump Dispenser, Pause, Drain Pump, End), the corresponding SSD (Seven Segment Display) value, and the steps for each component.  For example, for the Water Valve, the SSD value is 05 and the instruction is to turn on the water valve, the flow meter will allow 3L of water to enter the dishwasher.
Instructions on how to exit service mode: Disconnect power to the dishwasher.
Top display of a 24" dishwasher showing different wash cycles

Instructions to enter service mode: turn off the dishwasher, disconnect power, reapply power, press and hold Rinse and Start buttons within 60 seconds

Instructions to exit service mode: disconnect power to the dishwasher

Danger sign

Electrical Shock Hazard. Only authorized technicians should perform diagnostic voltage measurements. After performing voltage measurements, disconnect power before servicing. Failure to follow these instructions can result in death or electrical shock.
Failure Solutions

If an alarm occurs, the machine will enter a failure solving process: The buzzer sounds once per second for 30 seconds. The error code will be displayed. During that time, the drain pump will turn on for 2 minutes.
During the drain, if the door is opened, the drain timing will pause.

Table showing error codes, causes, and what to check.  It includes columns for Error Code (SSD*, NON-SSD*), error descriptions, causes, and steps to check the issue.  Rows detail specific error codes (E1, E3, E4, E6, E7) with their associated information.
*SSD: Seven Segment Display (Not available on 24" Front Display Dishwashers.)

Title: Voltage Measurement Safety Information. Instructions for performing live voltage measurements: Verify controls are off, allow enough space, keep safe distance, use proper equipment, disconnect power after measurements.

Title: IMPORTANT: Electrostatic Discharge (ESD) Sensitive Electronics. ESD problems are present everywhere.  Instructions to prevent ESD damage: Use an anti-static wrist strap or repeatedly touch a green ground connection point.

Additional instructions: Before removing parts, touch the anti-static bag to a green ground connection point. Avoid touching electronic parts, handle electronic control assembly by edges only, when repackaging observe above instructions.

Door Switch Circuit: Instructions on how to perform checks if the dishwasher does not detect the door open or closed. Items checked include harness/connection, door switch/latch assembly, and control board.

Test Procedure: Steps to check for improper installation, obstructions, or binding; unplug the dishwasher; remove access panel and control board cover; check door switch contacts and connections; disconnect connector CN6; measure across CN6 pins; check for loose connections; replace door switch if damaged; set voltmeter and connect leads; verify 12 VDC; reconnect CN6 and CN4 to control board; perform diagnostic cycle; reassemble all parts and panels.

Door Switch Strip Circuit diagram showing the components and connections of the door switch circuit. Includes heater relay, wash pump relays, drain pump relay, door switch sensing input, micro pins and CN6-1 and CN6-3 connectors.

Section: Component Testing with instructions on testing dishwasher components from the control board, including checks for poor connections, using a VOM or DVM for tests, checking connections before component replacement, and voltage and resistance checks.

Descriptions of each connector: CON1 (L1, N), CON2 (Drain & Wash Pump, with pin descriptions), PO1/PO2 (Heater), CN1 (Not used), CN2 (User Interface), CN3 (Turbidity Sensor), CN4 (Flow Meter/Thermistor/Rinse Aid Detector/Overflow Sw.), CN5 (Water Inlet Valve), CN6 (Dispenser and Door Switch), CN7 (Not used), CN8 (Not used).
Footer: 18" & 24" ADA Built-In Dishwashers. Page number: 3-7
Water Inlet Valve

Electrical Shock Hazard Only authorized technicians should perform diagnostic voltage measurements. After performing voltage measurements, disconnect power before servicing. Failure to follow these instructions can result in death or electrical shock.
Water Inlet Valve This test will check the wiring and components in the fill circuit. The following items are part of the fill circuit. Harness/Connection Water Inlet Valve Control Board

Test Procedure 1. Verify water supply is turned on and supply line is adequate. Check for water siphoning out of the dishwasher (improper drain connection). Check for debris in water line or fill valve inlet screen. Check for proper float switch operation. Repair as necessary. 2. Unplug dishwasher or disconnect power. 3. Remove access panel. Remove control board cover to access control board. 4. Unplug connector CN5 from control board. 5. Check the inlet valve and harness-using an ohmmeter, measure the resistance between CN5-1 and CN5-2. If the resistance is approximately 24 ohms, the water inlet valve and harness are good. Go to step 7.
Water Inlet Valve Strip Circuit

6. Reconnect CN5 to control board. 7. Set voltmeter to DC and connect lead black lead to CN5-1 (EV1) and red lead to CN5-2 (IS) on the control board. Plug in dishwasher or reconnect power. 8. Start the Service Mode Test and at step-1 measure for 12 VDC out of the control between CN5-1 and CN5-2. (Refer to the Water Inlet Valve Strip Circuit below.) IMPORTANT: The Water Inlet Valve must be connected to the control board to measure voltage accurately!!! If 12 VDC is not measured, replace the control board and retest. If 12 VDC is measured and water inlet valve is energized, go to step 9. 9. Check water level switch, harness, and connections. Replace or repair as necessary. 10. Unplug dishwasher or disconnect power. 11. Reassemble all parts and panels. 12. Plug in dishwasher or reconnect power and run Service Mode Test to verify repair.

Danger sign warning about electrical shock hazard. Only authorized technicians should perform diagnostic voltage measurements. Disconnect power before servicing. Failure to follow instructions can result in death or electrical shock.

Dispenser Circuit description. The detergent/rinse module automatically dispenses detergent and rinse agent at appropriate times. The module is activated five times during a wash cycle. This test checks wiring to the dispenser and dispenser solenoid. Items included are harness/connection, dispenser solenoid, and control board.
Diagram showing dispenser assembly. Includes parts like dispenser solenoid, rinse aid detection connector, and wiring.

Test procedure steps for servicing. Check for obstructions, unplug dishwasher, remove access panel, unplug connectors, check resistance, reconnect connectors, set voltmeter, start service mode test, unplug dishwasher, reassemble parts, and plug in dishwasher to verify repair.
Dispenser strip circuit diagram showing control board, dispenser, connectors (CN5-2 and CN6-2), voltage (12V), resistance (21 ohms), and ground (GND).
Important note: The dispenser must be connected to the control board to accurately measure voltage.

Danger sign warning about electrical shock hazard. Only authorized technicians should perform diagnostic voltage measurements, and disconnect power before servicing. Failure to follow these instructions can result in death or electrical shock.

Overview of the turbidity sensor: It's located under the tub, attached to the sump with two snap clips. During the pre-wash cycle, it senses the water and chooses the appropriate wash cycle. Note: If it's not functioning, the dishwasher will continue to operate but choose a default wash cycle.
Image showing the turbidity sensor location
Test procedure steps for checking the turbidity sensor and electrical connections.  It involves unplugging the dishwasher, removing access panels, testing voltage, and reassembling.
Diagram showing the strip circuit for the turbidity sensor

Description of the test: This test checks the wiring to the turbidity sensor, which measures the cloudiness or haziness of the water caused by suspended solids and particles.  Items included are harness/connection, turbidity sensor, and control board.

Wash Pump This test will check the wiring to the wash/rinse motor and the motor itself. The following items are part of the wash/rinse motor circuit. Harness/Connection Wash Pump & Capacitor Control Board

Test Procedure 1. Check the tub and sump for anything that may be impeding water flow. Inspect and if necessary clean the coarse-filter system. Also, inspect spray arm water nozzles and clean if needed. 2. Check the wash pump and electrical connections by performing the "Service Mode Test" on page 2-3. The following steps assume that this step was unsuccessful. 3. Unplug dishwasher or disconnect power. 4. Remove access panel. Remove control board cover to access control board. 5. Lay the dishwasher on its back. Use a small flat-blade screwdriver to unclip snaps around the drip-pan. Remove drip-pan/float switch to access under tub components. 6. Unplug connector CON1 and CON2 from control board.
Table showing Motor Coil, Pinouts, and Resistance for High and Low Coils
Steps 7-16 detailing troubleshooting steps for the wash pump, including checking resistance, connections, and the capacitor
Wash Pump Strip Circuit

For Service Technician Use Only
WARNING Electrical Shock Hazard Disconnect power before servicing. Replace all parts and panels before operating. Failure to do so can result in death or electrical shock.

Pressure Switch The pressure switch is an overfill safety device mounted on the base located on the right front. A clear plastic tube (the pressure switch hose) runs from the switch to the sump. As the dishwasher tub fills with water, the air pressure in the pressure switch hose increases. Normally, the control board regulates the amount of time the water fill valve remains open. If the water fill valve remains energized, the overfilling of the tub increases the air pressure in the pressure hose causing the pressure switch to close and energize the drain pump. This test will check the wiring to the pressure switch and the pressure switch itself. The following items are part of the pressure switch circuit. Harness/Connection Pressure Switch Pressure Switch Hose

Test Procedure 1. Unplug dishwasher or disconnect power. 2. Remove access panel. Remove control board cover to access control board. 3. Lay the dishwasher on its back. Use a small flat-blade screwdriver to unclip snaps around the drip-pan. Remove drip-pan/float switch to access under tub components. 4. Check hose connections between the pressure switch and sump. Ensure hose is routed correctly and not pinched or crimped. 5. Verify there is no debris in the hose. Check hose for leaks. Repair or replace as needed. 6. Check the following harnesses for continuity (refer to the following chart).

Pressure Switch Continuity Check Pressure Switch to CON1-2 (Brown Wire) Pressure Switch to CON2-1 (Red Wire) Pressure Switch to Drain Pump (Red Wire) If an open circuit is detected, check connections between control board, pressure switch, and drain pump. Repair or replace as necessary. If all harness test good, proceed to step 7.

Pressure Switch Continuity Check No Pressure Switch Open - Infinite or High Resistance Apply Pressure (Blow into hose*) Switch Closed - Continuity (less than 3 ohms) * When you blow into the pressure hose that is connected to the switch, an audible "click" can be heard indicating the switch has closed. If all pressure switch checks pass, continue to step 8. If the preceding checks did not pass, replace the Pressure Switch.
8. Reassemble all parts and panels. 9. Plug in dishwasher or reconnect power and retest.
Strip Circuit - Pressure Switch
Pressure Switch Strip Circuit

Section title: "Section 4: Component Access"
A brief description of the section's content: This section provides service parts access, removal, and installation instructions for the 18" & 24" ADA Built-In Dishwashers.

A list of components and their removal instructions, presented as a bulleted list.  It includes items such as: Removing the Tub Gasket and Trim, Removing the Outer Door Panel, Removing the Control Panel, and so on.  There are approximately 16 items in the list.
Footer containing page number and model information: "18" & 24" ADA Built-In Dishwashers    4-1"

Component Access Removing the Tub Gasket and Trim Remove the Tub Gasket and Trim The dishwasher tub seal prevents water leakage. The seal is fitted in a seal channel that lines the rim of the dishwasher tub. To remove the tub seal or trim 1. Open the dishwasher door. 2. Remove the dishwasher tub seal by grasping one end of the seal to peel it away from the seal channel (see Figure 1). 3. Remove the 2 screws (as indicated in Figure 2) and then pull the trim straight off the lip of the tub. 4. Reverse the above procedures to reinstall. NOTE: When installing the tub seal, make sure it is seated properly in the seal channel. Run your finger over the seal to make sure it is smooth and even for a proper seal. A correctly installed gasket will have both ends of the gasket equally distant from the bottom of the tub.
A picture showing how to remove the tub seal.
A picture showing how to remove the trim.

Component Access Removing the Outer Door Panel Warning message about electrical shock hazard. Disconnect power before servicing. Replace all parts and panels before operating. Failure to do so can result in death or electrical shock.

Remove Outer Door Panel 1. Unplug dishwasher or disconnect power. 2. Remove six (6) screws on the sides and top of door panel assembly (see Figure 1). 3. Then, lower the outer door panel from the control panel and remove (see Figure 2).
Figure 1: Image showing the top view of a dishwasher door panel with six screws highlighted, illustrating step 2 of removing the door panel.
Figure 2: Image showing the back view of a dishwasher with the outer door panel removed, showing step 3 of the process.
18" & 24" ADA Built-In Dishwashers 4-3 Page number and appliance specification.

Component Access Removing the Control Panel Warning Electrical Shock Hazard Disconnect power before servicing. Replace all parts and panels before operating. Failure to do so can result in death or electrical shock.

Removing the Control Panel 1. Unplug dishwasher or disconnect power. 2. Perform the procedures on page 4-2, "Remove Outer Door Panel" prior to performing the following steps. 3. Remove the eight (8) screws indicated in Figure 1. 4. Disconnect the User Interface harness and remove the handle cover. See Figure 2. 5. Remove the two (2) screws (indicated in Figure 2) to remove the User Interface PCBA. Disconnect UI Harness
Figure 1: Shows the location of eight screws on a dishwasher control panel.
Figure 2: Shows the location of two screws on a dishwasher user interface PCBA.

Component Access Removing Dispenser Assembly Warning
Electrical Shock Hazard Disconnect power before servicing. Replace all parts and panels before operating. Failure to do so can result in death or electrical shock.

Remove Dispenser Assembly 1. Unplug dishwasher or disconnect power. 2. Perform the procedures on page 4-2, "Remove Outer Door Panel" prior to performing the following steps. 3. Disconnect both dispenser harnesses (see Figure 1).
Figure 1
4. To remove the dispenser assembly, use a flat-blade screwdriver to move out the ten (10) snap clips, then push in to disengage from bracket. See Figure 2.
Figure 2
5. To reinstall the dispenser, press in the ten (10) snap clips inward as shown in Figure 3.
Figure 3
6. Press detergent dispenser inward into the bracket, ensuring all snap clips engage the assembly (see Figure 4).
Figure 4

Component Access. Removing the Air Breaker Assembly. Warning. Electrical Shock Hazard. Disconnect power before servicing. Replace all parts and panels before operating. Failure to do so can result in death or electrical shock.

Remove the Air Breaker Assembly. The Air breaker assembly is mounted on the left side of the tub. Its purpose is to provide a method of supplying water for the wash and rinse cycles. The air gap prevents the siphoning of wash water from flowing back into the water supply system and prevents the water pressure from not dropping below atmospheric pressure. The Air breaker assembly also allows air into the tub for improved drying. To remove the air breaker assembly: 1. Unplug dishwasher or disconnect power. 2. Remove the base side panel. 3. Disconnect flow meter harness from the air breaker assembly (see Figure 1).
Figure 1. Shows the air breaker assembly with a highlighted harness to disconnect.
Figure 2. Shows the air breaker assembly with highlighted hose clamps to remove.

4. Open the door and from inside the cabinet, rotate the air breaker nut and remove it from the air breaker assembly. 5. Pull away the air breaker assembly and remove the four (4) clamps securing the hoses to the assembly. See Figure 2.
Warnings about electrical shock hazard. Disconnect power before servicing and replace all parts and panels before operating to avoid death or electrical shock.

Steps to remove the nozzle duct: 1. Unplug dishwasher or disconnect power. 2. Open the door and remove the 2 screws on the lower spray seat (see Figure 1). 3. Release the snap clips (left side shown in Figure 2) and remove the nozzle duct and lower spray seat.
Image showing the location of two screws to be removed from the lower spray seat. Two arrows point to the screws.
Image showing the nozzle duct and snap clips. An arrow points to the snap clips.
Figure 1: Close-up view of the bottom of a dishwasher, showing the two screws to be removed.
Figure 2: Close-up view of the nozzle duct and snap clips.  Shows how to release the snap clips.
Title: Removing the Nozzle Duct

Component Access Removing the Control Board Warning Electrical Shock Hazard Disconnect power before servicing Replace all parts and panels before operating Failure to do so can result in death or electrical shock Remove the Control Board The Control Board can be removed from its enclosure at the bottom of the dishwasher 1 Unplug dishwasher or disconnect power 2 Remove two (2) screws securing the kick board (toe panel) to dishwasher frame (see Figure 1) 4 Disconnect all harnesses from the control board 5 Depress the two (2) clips indicated in Figure 3 and remove control board from enclosure

Figure 2 shows the control board enclosure with a cover screw and cover to be removed
Figure 3 shows the two clips on the control board that need to be depressed to remove it

Title: Removing the Water Valve. Warning message about electrical shock hazard. Instructions to unplug dishwasher or disconnect power before servicing and to replace all parts and panels before operating to prevent death or electrical shock.
Figure 1: Shows the location of two screws on the kick board and two screws on the insulating panel. Arrows point to the screws.
Instructions: Remove the two screws on the kick board (toe panel) and the two screws on the insulating panel (see Figure 1).
Figure 2: Shows the location of two screws that hold the water valve to the front brace. An arrow points to the screws.
Instructions: Remove the two screws that hold the water valve to the front brace (see Figure 2) and remove the fixing flat just like the picture below.
Figure 3: Shows how to disconnect the wire from the solenoid, remove the clamp, and remove the outlet hose from the water valve.
Instructions: Disconnect the wire from the solenoid (see Figure 3) and remove the clamp and the outlet hose from the water valve (see Figure 3).
Important note: The clamp is easily damaged during removal and should not be reused. Use the screw-type hose clamp provided with the new valve.
Title: Remove the Water Valve. Text explains the location of the water valve and provides steps for removing it.

Component Access. Removing the Pressure Switch. Warning: Electrical Shock Hazard. Disconnect power before servicing. Replace all parts and panels before operating. Failure to do so can result in death or electrical shock.

Remove the Pressure Switch. 1. Unplug dishwasher or disconnect power. 2. Open the dishwasher door and take out cutlery basket and lower rack. 3. Remove dishwasher from installation and lay dishwasher on its back. 4. Remove the one (1) screw that attaches base cover to the base. Remove base cover and set aside. 5. Release the snap clip that attaches the pressure switch to the base. Remove pressure switch from dishwasher.
Image showing the location of the pressure switch
6. Disconnect harness from pressure switch (see Figure 2). 7. Remove the pressure switch hose from the pressure switch. See Figure 2.
Image showing the pressure switch hose and harness
Note: When installing the pressure switch, ensure the switch is fully seated in the bottom of the base bracket.
Figure 1 showing the pressure switch location in the dishwasher
Figure 2 showing the pressure switch hose and harness

Component Access. Removing the Drain Pump. Warning: Electrical Shock Hazard. Disconnect power before servicing. Replace all parts and panels before operating. Failure to do so can result in death or electrical shock.

Remove the Drain Pump. 1. Unplug dishwasher or disconnect power. 2. Open the dishwasher door and take out cutlery basket and lower rack. 3. Remove dishwasher from installation and lay dishwasher on its back. 4. Remove the one (1) screw that attaches base cover to the base. Remove base cover and set aside. 5. Grasp drain pump and rotate 1/4 turn counterclockwise to remove (which the opposite direction of the mark on the sump). See Figure 2. 6. Disconnect wire harness from drain pump.
Image showing the location of the drain pump and how to remove it.
Image showing the location of the drain pump and how to remove it.
Note: Ensure the "O" Ring is retained in the drain pump before reassembly.
Figure 1 showing the drain pump.
Figure 2 showing how to disconnect the wire harness from the drain pump

Warnings about electrical shock hazard and the need to disconnect power before servicing. Also includes instructions to replace all parts and panels before operating to prevent death or electrical shock.

Step-by-step instructions on how to remove the turbidity switch, including unplugging the dishwasher, opening the door, removing the cutlery basket and lower rack, removing the dishwasher from its installation, removing a screw, disconnecting the wire harness, and releasing the snap clips.
A diagram showing the location of the turbidity sensor and wire harness. It shows how to disconnect the harness and release the snap clips.

Step-by-step instructions on how to remove the thermistor, including unplugging the dishwasher, opening the door, removing the cutlery basket and lower rack, removing the dishwasher from its installation, removing a screw, disconnecting the wire harness, and removing two screws securing the thermistor to the sump.
A diagram showing the location of the thermistor and wire harness. It shows how to disconnect the harness and remove the screws.
Note: Ensure the "O"Ring is retained in the Thermistor before reassembly.
Note: Ensure the "O"Ring is retained in the turbidity sensor before reassembly.

Component Access Removing the Heating Element Warning: Electrical Shock Hazard Disconnect power before servicing. Replace all parts and panels before operating. Failure to do so can result in death or electrical shock.

Remove the Heating Element 1. Unplug dishwasher or disconnect power. 2. Open the dishwasher door and take out cutlery basket and lower rack. 3. Remove dishwasher from installation and lay dishwasher on its back. 4. Remove the one (1) screw that attaches base cover to the base. Remove base cover and set aside. 5. Disconnect the two (2) pink wires from the heating element terminals (see Figure 1).
Figure 1: Shows the location of the heating terminals.
6. Remove the nuts and washers that attach the heating element to the bottom of the tub (see Figure 2). NOTE: Sump is removed to show location of heater terminals, nuts and washers.
Figure 2: Shows the location of the heater terminals, nuts and washers.
7. From the inside of the tub, lift the backside of the element and release it from the 2 retainers. See Figure 3.
Figure 3: Shows the location of the retainers.

Remove the Wash Pump Assembly 1 Unplug dishwasher or disconnect power 2 Open the dishwasher door and take out cutlery basket and lower rack 3 Remove dishwasher from installation and lay dishwasher on its back 4 Remove the one (1) screw that attaches base cover to the base Remove base cover and set aside 5 Remove wash pump outlet clamps and hose (see Figure 1) 6 Remove wash pump inlet clamps and hose (see Figure 1)
